tasks.title: Bug: Architect agent intermittently fails to write plan.json
tasks.description: The architect agent claims "plan written to .task/plan.json" in its output text but the file doesn't actually exist. Observed ~50% failure rate across orchestrator runs against the reify project. The orchestrator correctly detects this (artifacts.read_plan() returns empty) and blocks the task, but the wasted cost is significant ($1-2 per failed attempt). Workaround: reset task to pending and re-run. Root cause likely in how the agent uses the Write tool vs describing the plan in text output.
tasks.details: Observed during reify M1 orchestrator runs (2026-03-16). Task 1 failed in run 1, succeeded in run 2. Task 3 failed in run 3. The architect system prompt includes Write in --allowed-tools and instructs "Write the plan to .task/plan.json using the Write tool." The agent returns success with turns=15-32 and describes the plan in its result text, but the file is absent on disk. Possible causes: (1) agent describes the plan instead of calling Write, (2) Write tool call fails silently, (3) cwd mismatch between agent and worktree.

tasks.title: Bug: Verify step fails with `source: not found` on dash-based systems
tasks.description: verify.py uses asyncio.create_subprocess_shell() which invokes /bin/sh. On systems where /bin/sh is dash (e.g. Ubuntu), the `source` builtin doesn't exist — only `.` works. Verification commands like `source ~/.cargo/env && cargo test` fail with `/bin/sh: 1: source: not found`, causing tasks to exhaust verify attempts and block even when the code is correct.
tasks.details: Fix options: (1) Use `. ` instead of `source` in config commands (user-side workaround, already applied to reify config). (2) Pass executable='/bin/bash' to create_subprocess_shell in verify.py. (3) Document that verify commands must be POSIX-compatible. Option 2 is the proper fix since it doesn't burden config authors with shell compatibility knowledge.

tasks.title: Bug: Reviewers receive empty diff, rubber-stamp PASS
tasks.description: During the review stage, `git diff main...HEAD` sometimes returns an empty diff. All 5 reviewers then report "PASS — empty diff, no issues" which defeats the purpose of review. Observed on task 4 (reify-geometry) during reify M1 run.
tasks.details: Likely cause: the merge-base calculation `main...HEAD` returns nothing when the worktree branch has already been fast-forwarded or when main has moved. The review happens after verification passes, but if the worktree was rebased or the diff reference is stale, the diff is empty. Fix: compute the diff at worktree creation time (store the base commit in .task/metadata.json) and use that as the diff base, e.g. `git diff <base-sha>...HEAD`.

tasks.title: Bug: git add -A in worktrees commits .task/ artifacts
tasks.description: The orchestrator's git_ops.commit() runs `git add -A` which stages .task/plan.json, .task/iterations.jsonl, and .task/reviews/*.json. These internal orchestrator artifacts get committed to the task branch and merged to main. Workaround: add `.task/` to project .gitignore. Proper fix: exclude .task/ in git_ops.commit() or use a more targeted staging approach.
tasks.details: In git_ops.py line 61: `await _run(['git', 'add', '-A'], cwd=worktree)`. Fix options: (1) Add .task/ to a .gitignore that's created in the worktree during TaskArtifacts.init(). (2) Change git add -A to explicit file staging. (3) Run `git reset HEAD .task/` after `git add -A`. Option 1 is simplest — have artifacts.init() write a .task/.gitignore containing `*`.

tasks.title: Bug: Fused-memory task tools missing from Claude Code due to config path
tasks.description: When Claude Code connects to fused-memory via stdio, the default config (fused-memory/config/config.yaml) resolves the Taskmaster path as ./taskmaster-ai relative to the fused-memory directory, but taskmaster-ai lives at dark-factory/taskmaster-ai. Taskmaster init silently fails, causing all 11 task proxy tools to not be registered. The server logs a warning but the client has no way to know task tools are absent vs nonexistent.
tasks.details: The fix has two parts: (1) Project .mcp.json files should set CONFIG_PATH to a project-specific config with absolute Taskmaster paths, or set TASKMASTER_DIR env var. (2) The server should surface Taskmaster connection status to clients — e.g. include `taskmaster_connected: false` in get_status output, or mention it in the server instructions/capabilities so clients can detect the gap. Currently the failure is completely silent from the client's perspective.

tasks.title: Project scaffold, config, and app skeleton
tasks.description: Create the dashboard project structure under dashboard/ with all foundational files needed by subsequent tasks.
tasks.details:
Create the following files:

1. `dashboard/pyproject.toml` — hatchling build, dependencies: fastapi>=0.115, uvicorn[standard]>=0.34, jinja2>=3.1, httpx>=0.28, aiosqlite>=0.21. Dev extras: pytest>=8, pytest-asyncio>=0.25, httpx.

2. `dashboard/src/dashboard/__init__.py` — empty.

3. `dashboard/src/dashboard/config.py` — DashboardConfig dataclass with fields: host="127.0.0.1", port=8080, project_root=Path("/home/leo/src/dark-factory"), fused_memory_url="http://localhost:8000", fused_memory_project_id="dark_factory". Properties for derived paths: reconciliation_db (project_root/"fused-memory"/"data"/"reconciliation"/"reconciliation.db"), write_queue_db (project_root/"fused-memory"/"data"/"queue"/"write_queue.db"), tasks_json (project_root/".taskmaster"/"tasks"/"tasks.json"), worktrees_dir (project_root/".worktrees"). Support env var overrides prefixed DASHBOARD_ (e.g. DASHBOARD_PORT).

4. `dashboard/src/dashboard/app.py` — FastAPI app with: Jinja2Templates pointing to templates dir, StaticFiles mount at /static, lifespan context manager (creates httpx.AsyncClient stored in app.state), GET /api/health returning {"status": "ok"}. Do NOT add other routes yet — those come in tasks 5-8.

5. `dashboard/src/dashboard/data/__init__.py` — empty.

6. `dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/base.html` — Jinja2 base template. Dark theme (bg-gray-900, text-gray-100). Head includes: Tailwind CSS CDN (script tag), htmx CDN (script tag), Alpine.js CDN (script tag), link to /static/style.css. Body has a container div with {% block content %}{% endblock %}. Header with "Dark Factory" title.

7. `dashboard/src/dashboard/static/style.css` — minimal custom styles if needed beyond Tailwind utilities.

8. `dashboard/tests/__init__.py` — empty.

9. `dashboard/tests/conftest.py` — shared pytest fixtures: a fixture that creates a DashboardConfig with temp paths, a fixture for FastAPI TestClient.

Test strategy: Run `cd dashboard && uv sync && uv run pytest tests/ -x` — verify project installs cleanly and conftest fixtures work. Write a minimal test in conftest or a test_scaffold.py that imports dashboard.config and dashboard.app and asserts the health endpoint returns 200.

tasks.title: Memory data module
tasks.description: Create data/memory.py — the data access layer for memory health metrics from the fused-memory MCP HTTP server.
tasks.details:
Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/data/memory.py` with:

1. `async def mcp_tool_call(client: httpx.AsyncClient, base_url: str, tool_name: str, arguments: dict) -> dict` — POST JSON-RPC to `{base_url}/mcp` with body `{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "tools/call", "params": {"name": tool_name, "arguments": arguments}}`. Parse response: extract `result.content[0].text`, json.loads it, return dict. Timeout: 10s.

2. `async def get_memory_status(client: httpx.AsyncClient, config: DashboardConfig) -> dict` — call MCP tool `get_status` with `{"project_id": config.fused_memory_project_id}`. Returns dict with keys: graphiti (connected, node_count), mem0 (connected, memory_count), queue (counts dict, oldest_pending_age_seconds), taskmaster (connected). On httpx.ConnectError or httpx.TimeoutException, return `{"offline": True, "error": str(e)}`.

3. `async def get_queue_stats(client: httpx.AsyncClient, config: DashboardConfig) -> dict` — call MCP tool `get_queue_stats` with `{}`. Returns dict with counts (pending, in_flight, retry, completed, dead) and oldest_pending_age_seconds. Same error handling as above.

Create `dashboard/tests/test_memory.py` with tests:
- Test successful get_memory_status: mock httpx to return a valid MCP response, verify parsed output matches expected structure.
- Test successful get_queue_stats: same pattern.
- Test offline fallback: mock httpx to raise ConnectError, verify returns {"offline": True, ...}.
- Test malformed response: mock httpx to return unexpected JSON, verify graceful handling (returns empty dict or raises clear error).

Use httpx's MockTransport or pytest-httpx for mocking.

tasks.title: Reconciliation data module
tasks.description: Create data/reconciliation.py — async SQLite queries for reconciliation metrics from reconciliation.db (read-only).
tasks.details:
Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/data/reconciliation.py` with:

All functions open the database read-only: `aiosqlite.connect(f"file:{db_path}?mode=ro", uri=True)`.
All functions accept a `db_path: Path` parameter and handle FileNotFoundError by returning empty defaults.

1. `async def get_recent_runs(db_path: Path, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]` — query: `SELECT id, run_type, trigger_reason, started_at, completed_at, events_processed, status FROM runs ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT ?`. Return list of dicts. Calculate duration_seconds from completed_at - started_at for each run (both are ISO 8601 strings).

2. `async def get_watermarks(db_path: Path, project_id: str = "dark_factory") -> dict` — query: `SELECT * FROM watermarks WHERE project_id = ?`. Return dict with last_full_run_completed, last_episode_timestamp, last_memory_timestamp, last_task_change_timestamp. Return empty dict if no row.

3. `async def get_buffer_stats(db_path: Path) -> dict` — queries: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_buffer WHERE status = 'buffered'` and `SELECT MIN(timestamp) FROM event_buffer WHERE status = 'buffered'`. Return `{"buffered_count": int, "oldest_event_age_seconds": float|None}`. Calculate age from oldest timestamp vs now.

4. `async def get_burst_state(db_path: Path) -> list[dict]` — query: `SELECT agent_id, state, last_write_at, burst_started_at FROM burst_state`. Return list of dicts.

5. `async def get_latest_verdict(db_path: Path) -> dict | None` — query: `SELECT run_id, severity, action_taken, reviewed_at FROM judge_verdicts ORDER BY reviewed_at DESC LIMIT 1`. Return dict or None.

Create `dashboard/tests/test_reconciliation.py`:
- Fixture: create an in-memory SQLite db, execute CREATE TABLE statements matching the reconciliation.db schema (watermarks, runs, event_buffer, burst_state, judge_verdicts tables — see PRD for exact schemas), insert sample rows.
- Test each query function against the fixture DB.
- Test missing database file returns empty defaults.
- Test empty tables return empty lists/dicts/None.

SQLite schemas for the fixture (from the actual codebase):
- `CREATE TABLE watermarks (project_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, last_full_run_id TEXT, last_full_run_completed TEXT, last_episode_timestamp TEXT, last_memory_timestamp TEXT, last_task_change_timestamp TEXT)`
- `CREATE TABLE runs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, project_id TEXT NOT NULL, run_type TEXT NOT NULL, trigger_reason TEXT NOT NULL, started_at TEXT NOT NULL, completed_at TEXT, events_processed INTEGER DEFAULT 0, stage_reports TEXT DEFAULT '{}', status TEXT DEFAULT 'running')`
- `CREATE TABLE event_buffer (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, project_id TEXT NOT NULL, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, event_source TEXT NOT NULL, agent_id TEXT, timestamp TEXT NOT NULL, payload TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'buffered')`
- `CREATE TABLE burst_state (agent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle', last_write_at TEXT NOT NULL, burst_started_at TEXT)`
- `CREATE TABLE judge_verdicts (run_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, reviewed_at TEXT NOT NULL, severity TEXT NOT NULL, findings TEXT DEFAULT '[]', action_taken TEXT DEFAULT 'none')`

tasks.title: Orchestrator discovery module
tasks.description: Create data/orchestrator.py — process scanning, task tree parsing, and worktree artifact reading for orchestrator status.
tasks.details:
Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/data/orchestrator.py` with:

1. `def find_running_orchestrators() -> list[dict]` — run `subprocess.run(["ps", "aux"], capture_output=True, text=True)`. For each line containing "orchestrator" and "--prd", extract PID (field 1), start time (fields 8-9), and PRD path (regex `--prd\s+(\S+)`). Return list of `{"pid": int, "prd": str, "running": True, "started": str}`. Filter out the grep/ps process itself.

2. `def load_task_tree(tasks_json_path: Path) -> list[dict]` — read and parse `.taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json`. Extract `data["master"]["tasks"]` (or `data["tasks"]` — handle both formats). Return list of task dicts with keys: id, title, status, priority, dependencies, and metadata (if present, includes modules). Handle FileNotFoundError → return []. Handle malformed JSON → log warning, return [].

3. `def read_task_artifacts(worktree_path: Path) -> dict` — Read orchestrator artifacts from a worktree's `.task/` directory:
   - `.task/metadata.json`: parse for task_id, title, base_commit, created_at
   - `.task/plan.json`: parse steps array, count done vs total → derive phase (PLAN if no plan, EXECUTE/VERIFY/REVIEW/MERGE based on step progress, DONE if all done)
   - `.task/iterations.jsonl`: count lines (don't parse each line)
   - `.task/reviews/*.json`: glob, parse each, count PASS vs ISSUES_FOUND → return summary like "3/5 PASS"
   All wrapped in try/except, return None/0/"—" for missing files.
   Returns: `{"metadata": dict|None, "phase": str, "plan_progress": {"done": int, "total": int}, "iteration_count": int, "review_summary": str}`.

4. `def discover_orchestrators(config: DashboardConfig) -> list[dict]` — combine process scan + worktree scan:
   - Get running orchestrators from find_running_orchestrators()
   - Get task tree from load_task_tree(config.tasks_json)
   - Scan config.worktrees_dir for subdirectories, read artifacts from each
   - For each running orchestrator: attach the task tree and per-task artifacts
   - Compute summary stats: total, done, in_progress, blocked, pending counts
   - Return list of orchestrator dicts with all combined data

Create `dashboard/tests/test_orchestrator.py`:
- Test find_running_orchestrators: mock subprocess.run to return canned ps output with orchestrator lines. Verify PID and PRD extraction.
- Test load_task_tree: create a temp tasks.json with sample data. Verify parsing. Test missing file. Test malformed JSON.
- Test read_task_artifacts: create temp dir with .task/ subdirectory containing sample metadata.json, plan.json (with mix of done/pending steps), iterations.jsonl (3 lines), reviews/reviewer-1.json (PASS), reviews/reviewer-2.json (ISSUES_FOUND). Verify all fields parsed correctly.
- Test discover_orchestrators: mock process scan, create temp worktrees dir with one worktree containing artifacts. Verify combined output.

tasks.title: Memory health cards partial and route
tasks.description: Create the memory health cards HTML partial template and its FastAPI route, wiring data from the memory data module.
tasks.details:
Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/partials/memory.html`:

Four cards in a horizontal flex row (flex, gap-4). Each card: bg-gray-800, rounded-lg, p-6, shadow.

1. **Graphiti card**: Status dot (green if connected, red if not — use a 3x3 rounded-full div with bg-green-500/bg-red-500). "Graphiti" label (text-sm text-gray-400). Node count as large number (text-3xl font-bold text-white). Subtitle "knowledge graph nodes" (text-xs text-gray-500).

2. **Mem0 card**: Same pattern. Memory count. Subtitle "vector memories".

3. **Taskmaster card**: Same dot pattern. "Taskmaster" label. Text "Connected" or "Disconnected" (instead of a number).

4. **Write Queue card**: Tri-state dot: green if all counts are 0, yellow if pending > 0, red if dead > 0. "Write Queue" label. Show three metrics inline: "N pending · N retry · N dead" with dead count in red text if > 0. If oldest_pending_age_seconds is not None and > 60, show "oldest: Xm ago" in yellow text.

If the data has `offline: True`, show a single full-width card: "Fused Memory Server Offline" with red styling and the error message.

Add to `dashboard/src/dashboard/app.py`:
- `GET /partials/memory` async route: get httpx client from request.app.state, call get_memory_status() and get_queue_stats() from dashboard.data.memory, render templates/partials/memory.html with the data as template context, return HTMLResponse.
- Import the data functions at the top of app.py.

Test strategy: verify the route returns 200 with text/html content-type. Test with mock data module returning both online and offline states. Check that the HTML contains expected elements (e.g., "Graphiti", "Mem0", node count value).

tasks.title: Reconciliation panel partial and route
tasks.description: Create the reconciliation panel HTML partial template and its FastAPI route, wiring data from the reconciliation data module.
tasks.details:
Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/partials/recon.html`:

Two-column grid layout (grid grid-cols-2 gap-6). Dark themed cards (bg-gray-800 rounded-lg p-6).

**Left column — Current State:**

- **Event Buffer** section: Show "N events buffered". If oldest_event_age_seconds is not None, show "oldest: Xm Xs ago". Use yellow text if age > 300s.

- **Burst State** table: columns agent_id, state. State "bursting" in orange badge, "idle" in gray badge. Show "No agents" if empty.

- **Watermarks** section: Show each timestamp as relative time (implement a Jinja2 filter `timeago` that converts ISO 8601 to "Xm ago" / "Xh ago" / "Xd ago" relative to now). Fields: Last full run, Last episode, Last memory, Last task change. Use text-gray-400 for labels, text-white for values.

- **Latest Verdict**: Severity badge — bg-green-600 for "ok", bg-yellow-600 for "minor", bg-orange-600 for "moderate", bg-red-600 for "serious". Show action_taken next to it. Show "No verdicts yet" if None.

**Right column — Recent Runs (last 5):**

Table with columns: Trigger, Type, Status, Events, Duration, When.
- Status badges: bg-green-600=completed, bg-blue-600=running, bg-red-600=failed, bg-orange-600=rolled_back, bg-purple-600=circuit_breaker.
- Duration: show in seconds or "—" if still running.
- When: relative time (timeago filter).
- Show "No reconciliation runs yet" if empty.

Add to `dashboard/src/dashboard/app.py`:
- `GET /partials/recon` async route: call all 5 reconciliation query functions (get_recent_runs, get_watermarks, get_buffer_stats, get_burst_state, get_latest_verdict) with config.reconciliation_db and render the partial.
- Register the `timeago` Jinja2 filter on the templates environment.

The `timeago` filter: accept an ISO 8601 string, parse with datetime.fromisoformat, compute delta from now (UTC), return human string like "3m ago", "2h ago", "1d ago". Handle None → return "never".

Test strategy: verify route returns 200. Test with mock reconciliation data. Check HTML contains expected badges and table structure.

tasks.title: Orchestrator list and task detail partials and routes
tasks.description: Create the orchestrator list and task detail HTML partial templates and their FastAPI routes, wiring data from the orchestrator discovery module.
tasks.details:
Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/partials/orchestrators.html`:

**Header**: "Orchestrators" (text-xl font-bold) + count of running instances as badge (bg-blue-600 rounded-full px-2 text-sm).

**Per-orchestrator card** (bg-gray-800 rounded-lg p-6 mb-4):
- **Header line**: PID with green "running" badge (bg-green-600 text-xs rounded px-2 py-0.5) or gray "completed" badge. PRD filename (extracted from path, text-gray-400).
- **Progress bar**: Full-width bar (h-3 rounded-full bg-gray-700 overflow-hidden). Colored segments inside as flex children: green (done), blue (in-progress), yellow (pending), red (blocked). Width proportional to count/total.
- **Stats row**: Flex with gap, each stat: count + label. "N done · N in-progress · N blocked · N pending" with matching colors.
- **Expand button**: "Show tasks" / "Hide tasks" toggle using Alpine.js `@click="open = !open"` and `x-show="open"`. Wrap card in `<div x-data="{ open: false }">`.

Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/partials/tasks.html`:

**Task table** (inside the expandable section, rendered per orchestrator):
- Table with classes: w-full text-sm text-left text-gray-300.
- Header row: ID, Title, Status, Phase, Iterations, Reviews, Modules (bg-gray-700 text-gray-400 text-xs uppercase).
- Data rows: 
  - **Status** as badge: bg-green-600=done, bg-blue-600=in-progress, bg-yellow-600=pending, bg-red-600=blocked, bg-gray-600=cancelled/deferred.
  - **Phase**: short label (PLAN/EXEC/VERIFY/REVIEW/MERGE/DONE/—) with muted color.
  - **Iterations**: number or "—".
  - **Reviews**: e.g. "3/5 PASS" or "—".
  - **Modules**: comma-joined, text-xs text-gray-500, truncated with `truncate max-w-[200px]`.

If no orchestrators found, show a centered message: "No orchestrators detected" with gray text.

Add to `dashboard/src/dashboard/app.py`:
- `GET /partials/orchestrators` async route: call discover_orchestrators(config), render orchestrators.html with the orchestrator list. The tasks.html partial is included inline within orchestrators.html (use Jinja2 include or render tasks directly in the template loop).

Test strategy: verify route returns 200. Test with mock discover_orchestrators returning both empty list and a list with one running + one completed orchestrator. Check HTML contains progress bar, stats, task table elements.

tasks.title: Full page assembly, entry point, and integration tests
tasks.description: Create index.html combining all partials with htmx polling, add __main__.py entry point, and write integration tests for all routes.
tasks.details:
Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/index.html`: Extends base.html. Content block contains 4 sections stacked vertically (space-y-8 max-w-7xl mx-auto p-6): 1. **Memory Health** section: `
Loading...
` 2. **Reconciliation** section: `
Loading...
` 3. **Orchestrators** section: `
Loading...
` 4. **Footer**: Small text "Dark Factory Dashboard" with current time (updated via Alpine.js `x-text` with a setInterval, or just static). Add to `dashboard/src/dashboard/app.py`: - `GET /` route: render index.html (no data needed — partials load themselves via htmx). Create `dashboard/src/dashboard/__main__.py`: ```python """Entry point: python -m dashboard.app""" import uvicorn from dashboard.config import DashboardConfig config = DashboardConfig() uvicorn.run("dashboard.app:app", host=config.host, port=config.port, reload=True) ``` Create `dashboard/tests/test_app.py` — integration tests using FastAPI TestClient: 1. **Test GET /** — returns 200, content-type text/html, body contains "Dark Factory", contains hx-get="/partials/memory", hx-get="/partials/recon", hx-get="/partials/orchestrators". 2. **Test GET /partials/memory** — mock dashboard.data.memory functions to return sample data. Returns 200, text/html, contains "Graphiti", "Mem0", "Taskmaster", "Write Queue". 3. **Test GET /partials/memory offline** — mock to return {"offline": True}. Returns 200, contains "Offline". 4. **Test GET /partials/recon** — mock dashboard.data.reconciliation functions. Returns 200, contains expected table elements. 5. **Test GET /partials/recon no data** — mock to return empty defaults. Returns 200, contains "No reconciliation runs". 6. **Test GET /partials/orchestrators** — mock dashboard.data.orchestrator.discover_orchestrators to return a sample orchestrator with tasks. Returns 200, contains progress bar elements, task table. 7. **Test GET /partials/orchestrators empty** — mock to return []. Returns 200, contains "No orchestrators detected". 8. **Test GET /api/health** — returns 200, application/json, body is {"status": "ok"}. Use `unittest.mock.patch` or `monkeypatch` to mock the data modules. Import the app and create TestClient in a fixture. Test strategy: `cd dashboard && uv run pytest tests/ -x -v`. All 8+ tests should pass. Also manually verify by running `cd dashboard && uv run python -m dashboard.app` and opening http://127.0.0.1:8080 in a browser.

tasks.title: Bug: Task reconciliation uses project_root as memory project_id
tasks.description: Task-triggered reconciliation currently emits events keyed by Taskmaster's `project_root` and then reuses that string as the memory `project_id`. That causes completion knowledge, memory hints, and related searches to land under a different Graphiti group / Mem0 collection than the rest of the project's memory, fragmenting the knowledge base.
tasks.details: The repo docs distinguish task routing (`project_root="/home/leo/src/dark-factory"`) from memory routing (`project_id="dark_factory"`), but the reconciliation path collapses them. Relevant code paths: `fused_memory.middleware.task_interceptor.TaskInterceptor._make_event`, `fused_memory.reconciliation.targeted.TargetedReconciler`, and `fused_memory.models.scope.Scope`. Fix should establish a canonical mapping from task project root to memory project_id and use it consistently across targeted and full reconciliation stages.
tasks.testStrategy: Add integration tests proving a task status transition writes memory into the canonical project scope (`dark_factory`) rather than the filesystem path. Cover both targeted reconciliation and any stage-base task tools that currently pass `project_root=project_id`.

tasks.title: Bug: add_episode returns an episode_id that is never written to Graphiti
tasks.description: MemoryService generates and returns an `episode_id` immediately from `add_episode()`, but that UUID is not included in the queued Graphiti write. The actual Graphiti episode therefore gets a different UUID, so the returned ID cannot be used reliably for provenance, follow-up reads, or deletion.
tasks.details: Relevant paths: `fused_memory.services.memory_service.MemoryService.add_episode`, `_execute_graphiti_write`, and `fused_memory.backends.graphiti_client.GraphitiBackend.add_episode`. The durable queue payload needs to carry the generated UUID through to Graphiti, or the API contract needs to change so the system does not promise a stable episode ID before the backend has accepted one.
tasks.testStrategy: Add a test that enqueues an episode, drains the queue, and asserts the returned `episode_id` matches the UUID passed into the Graphiti backend. Also cover delete/provenance flows that depend on the returned ID.

tasks.title: Bug: Unified Graphiti search/delete contract is internally inconsistent
tasks.description: The fused-memory API presents search results and deletion as a uniform cross-store contract, but Graphiti results currently return edge UUIDs with no category while `delete_memory(store="graphiti")` expects an episode UUID. Category filtering also silently drops all Graphiti results because their category is always null.
tasks.details: Relevant paths: `fused_memory.services.memory_service._search_graphiti`, `search(... categories=...)`, `delete_memory()`, and the MCP tool docs in `fused_memory.server.tools`. This needs either a real unified identifier contract or explicit store-specific semantics. At minimum the API/docs/tests should agree on what Graphiti IDs represent and whether category-filtered searches can include Graphiti facts.
tasks.testStrategy: Add coverage for: (1) Graphiti search results used as delete inputs, (2) category-filtered searches that include Graphiti results when appropriate, and (3) tool docs / response schema consistency around `memory_id`, `source_store`, and category handling.

tasks.title: Bug: EventBuffer quiescence check reads the wrong durable queue stats shape
tasks.description: Reconciliation's quiescence logic expects top-level `pending`, `retry`, and `in_flight` fields from the durable write queue, but `DurableWriteQueue.get_stats()` returns those counts nested under `counts`. That can make the system think the queue is idle and trigger reconciliation while Graphiti writes are still outstanding.
tasks.details: Relevant paths: `fused_memory.reconciliation.event_buffer.EventBuffer._is_quiescent` and `fused_memory.services.durable_queue.DurableWriteQueue.get_stats`. The current tests mock the flat shape instead of the real one, so the mismatch passes unnoticed. Fix the consumer, then align the tests with the actual queue contract.
tasks.testStrategy: Add a regression test that wires `EventBuffer` to a real or realistically mocked `DurableWriteQueue.get_stats()` response and proves quiescence stays false while `counts.pending + counts.retry + counts.in_flight > 0`.
